prompt:
Build a Philatelic Museum Operations Suite that unifies collection discovery, exhibit planning, on-site program scheduling, and staffing workflows in a single offline-ready web application. Visitors and floor staff use a React-based interface for fast search and guided navigation: users can run combined searches across stamp titles, catalog numbers, artists, series, and time periods with fuzzy matching, filters by category and tags, sortable results, and paginated browsing capped at 50 items per page for performance. Autocomplete and “hot keyword” suggestions are curated by curators in the admin console to reflect current exhibits and seasonal themes. Curators manage a knowledge-graph view that links stamp–masterpiece–artist–country/period–series relationships with configurable weights (0–100) and rule-based constraints, and the UI highlights duplicates, circular references, and orphan nodes before changes can be published. Exhibit managers define venue–hall–zone–display case hierarchies and visually orchestrate visitor routes, including sequence rules, optional branches, and accessibility detours; each route produces a printable itinerary with estimated walk time based on default pacing of 3 mph and configurable dwell times per case. Program coordinators schedule group classes and personal training sessions for docents and educators with coach profiles, availability windows, capacity limits, and waitlists; cancellations within 12 hours are marked late-cancel and deduct one session credit, while no-shows deduct two credits, with all notifications delivered as in-app inbox messages and optional printouts to avoid any dependency on external services. Employers (internal departments) post part-time roles for events and exhibits with drafts, approvals, takedowns for policy violations, and appeals, so staffing can be planned without spreadsheets.

The backend uses Express to enforce business rules, validate inputs, and expose REST-style APIs consumed by the decoupled React frontend, while MongoDB persists all records locally, including audit trails and reporting artifacts. Authentication is strictly username and password with minimum 12 characters, complexity checks, 5-attempt lockout for 15 minutes, and 30-minute idle session expiry; RBAC defines Administrator, Curator, Exhibit Manager, Program Coordinator, Employer, Reviewer, and Auditor roles, with sensitive actions (publishing relationships, changing route rules, approving jobs, exporting data) requiring re-entry of the current password and producing immutable audit logs retained for 7 years. Analytics provides configurable metric/dimension models and self-service dashboards backed by stored report definitions; scheduled runs generate local files at 2:00 AM and place them in a managed reconciliation folder with checksum fingerprints, while anomaly monitoring flags thresholds such as “bookings drop >30% week-over-week” directly on the dashboard and in the in-app inbox. Exports to CSV/JSON apply permission checks and masking (for example, only last 4 digits of phone numbers and redacted notes fields), and search caching is implemented with MongoDB TTL collections to keep frequently used queries responsive during busy exhibit hours.
